mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling or remains below recent highs, equity discount rates ease and risk appetite improves, lifting S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is valid only if rate cuts are not being driven by acute recession stress.
failure_condition: Reject if 20d forward SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of periods when FEDFUNDS is below its 6-month moving average, or if the effect turns negative during easing cycles tied to recessionary spikes in labor weakness.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent trend signals labor-market deterioration and weaker household income growth, which should depress near-term S&P 500 returns as earnings expectations are marked down.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20d SPX returns in more than 45% of cases, or if the average forward return after the trigger is not below the unconditional mean by at least 1 standard error.

mechanism_narrative: Faster retail sales growth in RSAFS implies stronger current consumption and revenue momentum, which should support S&P 500 returns over the next month through upward earnings revisions.
failure_condition: Reject if positive month-over-month RSAFS changes do not coincide with above-baseline 20d SPX returns, or if the sign flips negative in more than two consecutive 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous pickup in INDPRO and HOUST indicates broad real activity and capacity utilization, which should translate into stronger equity returns over the next quarter through improved industrial earnings and cyclical sentiment.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and HOUST both rise over the prior 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if the joint-upturn trigger does not outperform either series alone, or if triggered 60d SPX returns are not positive in at least 60% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in CPIAUCSL after a period of elevated inflation lowers expected policy pressure and real yield risk, supporting S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days via multiple expansion.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation rate falls for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: Reject if three-month disinflation triggers do not precede higher-than-baseline 20d SPX returns, or if the mean forward return after the trigger is not statistically above zero.

mechanism_narrative: A high FEDFUNDS rate above 4.5% tightens financial conditions and raises discount rates; when inflation remains sticky, this pressure should weigh on S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and CPIAUCSL inflation remains elevated
failure_condition: Reject if the joint high-rate/high-inflation regime does not deliver negative 20d SPX returns in at least 55% of occurrences, or if the interaction adds no predictive value over FEDFUNDS alone.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in PAYEMS signals weakening payroll growth and slower income formation, which should reduce consumer spending expectations and pressure S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if 3-month payroll declines are not followed by below-average 60d SPX returns, or if the predictive sign is positive in a majority of late-cycle observations.

mechanism_narrative: When GDP growth in GDPC1 is firm while unemployment stays low, the market should price a soft-landing expansion and deliver positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal fails if GDP strength is accompanied by rising unemployment, which converts growth into a stagflation-like regime.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth is positive and UNRATE is not rising
failure_condition: Reject if the joint growth-low-unemployment condition does not produce positive 20d SPX returns in at least 60% of cases, or if returns are weak when GDPC1 is strong but UNRATE rises.
